title: Tiberian Factions for Red Alert
description:
[i]My son asked me who would win if GDI battled the Soviets. Now we can find out.[/i]

[h2]Graphics[/h2]
[b]To be played on HD Graphics mode only. New theatre terrain and some units do not render in Classic mode very well.[/b]

[h2]Features[/h2]
[list]
[*] Adds GDI and Nod alongside the Allies and Soviets with full TD logic. Ion cannons, flame tanks, stealth tanks, Nod Airstrip, Refinery docking - all work.
[*] UI shows GDI / Nod logos on Skirmish select screen.
[*] Full Tiberium ecosystem and TD skirmish maps. (The first time you load the mod you will need to run a skirmish match for the maps to appear in the Custom tab).
[*] GDI / Nod rudimentary skirmish ai implemented.
[*] TD EVA, unit voices and Radar on/off sounds all work.
[*] Skirmish music will play a mix of RA and TD music tracks.
[/list]

[h2]Changelog[/h2]
[b]Version 2.1.0[/b]
[list]
[*] Rally points: select a production building or repair bay and click the ground to set where its units go. Alt+Click rallies onto a unit, clicking the building clears it.
[*] Harvesters spread across refineries and pick the dock with the shortest wait.
[*] Units queue for a busy repair bay, and repaired units drive off instead of blocking the pad.
[*] 11 zoom levels instead of 8, including further zoom-out.
[/list]
[b][u]Version 2.0.0[/u][/b]
[list]
[*] Tiberium ecosystem added. Tiberium will spread, convert trees into blossom trees and harm infantry. There is a small chance that an infantry unit dying of Tiberium poisoning will spawn a Visceroid. 
[*] 31-map Tiberian Dawn map pack: every TD skirmish map, across temperate, winter, and desert theatres. Maps appear under Custom Maps after your first skirmish match with the mod. This is a workaround as I have not found a way to add maps to the official list without replacing existing maps.
[*] Tiberian Dawn terrain in full HD, including winter and desert theatres.
[*] Snowy trees on the winter maps, using TD's own winter tree art.
[/list]
[b]Version 1.1.6[/b]
[list]
[*] Streamlined the main menu: removed "Start New Game" and moved "Mission Select" to the top. Start New Game opened Red Alert's original campaign picker, a screen this mod can't customise; everything is now reached through Mission Select.
[/list]
[b]Version 1.1.5[/b]
[list]
[*] Fixed: in the skirmish lobby, the flag pinned to your chosen start position on the map preview showed the old country flag instead of the GDI eagle / Nod scorpion. It now matches your faction.
[/list]
[b]Version 1.1.4[/b]
[list]
[*] Fixed: in skirmish, if you picked your own start location but left AI players unpicked, an AI could sometimes spawn on the same spot as you. Every player now gets a distinct start position.
[/list]
[b]Version 1.1.3[/b]
[list]
[*] Fixed: GDI and Nod units (vehicles, infantry, and aircraft) built about 40% slower than equal-cost Allied/Soviet units; they now build at the same speed. Building construction times are unchanged.
[/list]
[b]Version 1.1.2[/b]
[list]
[*] Fixed: the GDI/Nod Construction Yard was stretched and bulged out of its foundation; it now displays at the correct size and sits properly in place.
[*] Smaller download: the mod's program file is about 25 MB smaller (debug data that did nothing in-game has been stripped). No gameplay change.
[/list]
[b]Version 1.1.1[/b]
[list]
[*] Fixed: GDI and Nod unit and building sight ranges. The Hum-vee scout in particular could barely see past the shroud.
[*] Fixed: the Tiberium Harvester now drives off to harvest on its own when built from the war factory, instead of sitting idle.
[*] Fixed: GDI and Nod now get the build-speed bonus from owning a second factory, the same as the Allies and Soviets.
[*] Fixed: the radar online/offline sound no longer repeats endlessly.
[*] Balance: the GDI APC was far too fast and outran the Hum-vee scout; brought down to a sensible transport speed.
[/list]
[b]Version 1.1.0[/b]
[list]
[*] Fixed: GDI and Nod now start skirmishes with their own Tiberian Dawn units, instead of borrowed Allied tanks and infantry.
[*] Balance: the GDI/Nod Harvester now moves at the same speed as the Allied/Soviet Harvester. It previously used its slower Tiberian Dawn values, which put GDI and Nod at an early-economy disadvantage.
[/list]
[b]Version 1.0.0[/b]
[list]
[*] Initial release: GDI and Nod as fully playable factions with their own bases, full unit rosters, superweapons, working AI opponents, and authentic Tiberian Dawn voices and sound.
[/list]

[h2]Planned[/h2]
[list]
[*] [b]Red Alert balance pass[/b]: the factions currently use Tiberian Dawn authentic stats; a future update will tune them for Red Alert's scale and pacing.
[*] [b]Smarter AI[/b]: ongoing improvements to how the computer opponents build, defend, and use their armies and superweapons.
[*] [b]More QoL features[/b]: the next wave of CFE Patch Redux adoptions, including attack-move and improved unit pathfinding (this is a DLL mod, so CFE Patch itself can't run alongside it).
[/list]

[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[list]
[*] This is a [b]DLL mod[/b]. It will not work alongside any other mod that also modifies RedAlert.dll. Disable other DLL mods such as CFE Patch before enabling this one.
[*] Single-player skirmish tested. LAN does work but I have experienced crashes with crates and have not yet investigated this. Recommend turning crates off for LAN play.
[/list]

[h2]Known limitations[/h2]
[list]
[*] The [b]Select-all-combat-units (A)[/b] and [b]Deploy (/)[/b] keyboard shortcuts only recognise the Allied/Soviet Harvester and MCV, not the GDI/Nod ones. This is a limitation in the game's launcher that a mod cannot reach. To deploy a GDI/Nod MCV, select it and click it (the deploy cursor still works). The A key will also grab GDI/Nod harvesters and MCVs, so use a drag-box to select your army instead.
[*] Remastered graphics are recommended. Classic graphics will not work for new theatre tiles imported from TD.
[/list]

[h2]Source and licensing[/h2]
GitHub: https://github.com/lukegibson101/cnc-ra-tiberian-factions
Built on top of [url=https://github.com/TheAssemblyArmada/Vanilla-Conquer]Vanilla Conquer[/url]. DLL source is [b]GPL v3[/b], inherited from EA's 2020 Remastered Collection source release.

[h2]Acknowledgements and inspiration[/h2]
This project doesn't bundle these mods, but their work shaped how we approached the engine. Thanks to:
[list]
[*] [b]Reilsss[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2853520457]Reilsss's Command & Conquer in Red Alert[/url], inspiration for reimagining Red Alert's factions as GDI and Nod, and for how to bring TD's sounds across.
[*] [b]DontCryJustDie[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3003163891]TD-Assets[/url], TD art and audio surfaced into the RA engine.
[*] [b]JohnnyJigglez[/b]: [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/commandandconquerremastered/mods/21]EMC (Enhanced Modding Capabilities)[/url], INI-driven extensibility reference.
[*] [b]ChthonVII[/b]: [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2268301299]CFE Patch Redux[/url] (with cfehunter and Root-Core), source of the 2.1.0 rally points, harvester, repair bay and zoom features (GPL v3).
[/list]

Not endorsed by or affiliated with Electronic Arts.
